reviews.review_msg:
The lectures are pretty dry and boring, especially at 7:30 pm. The lectures weren't even necessary because the slides and recordings are posted. 2 tests worth 40%, but if you memorize the slides and the textbook (which is really short), you'll do well. 

Prof was really nice and tried to make the class as interesting as it could be.

Course is pretty dry and lectures are very difficult to sit through. All content is based on a textbook so as long as you read/take notes on it you can do well. Prof seems pretty nice but it can be very hard to pay attention as its not very interesting. Make sure you know the stuff that overlaps between lectures and the textbook and you'll do well.

You can tell she's kind and accommodating, which I think is important. Tests aren't too difficult and she posts lecture videos which is nice for if you can't make it to class all the time. Only down side is her lectures drag on and can be quite confusing, as she jumps around to different topics/repeats things often.
ruined my semester trying to listen to her yapping bruh. even if there's no other 300 levels to take just take the L over taking a class with her ever

she has no idea what shes doing. run away
run fast run far

Graded only off of two things, she seems like a nice person but the lectures are all over the place its impossible to keep up as ideas don't seem to be connected. 3 hour lectures were so boring. don't take this if you dont need to.

Rachel is a wonderful prof. However, her teaching of the course was based on the textbook and it doesn't seem like she knew what she was teaching besides follow what the book author wrote. The grading breakdown of the class was unclear. 2 quizzes, 1 test, 1 paper, some in class activities! She buys us timbits after a quiz so thats a plus!

only read the book. Weekly quizzes were easy and fair. Most questions came from the textbook lol. Prof is very understanding and didnt want to stress us out so she gave many extensions and reduce the workload for us.

lectures arent helpful, the tas are, but honestly i only relied on the textbook for this class. I got 100% on every assignment except for the last few where I fell behind on the readings due to other classes workloads. Her test and assignment questions are very fair. She is understanding and gave many extensions to everyone when i asked for one

SO, go to your TA tutorials. That is how I passed. Elizabeth is AMAZING and keep up with the weekly quizzes. Annoying with auto grading for online but shes really not bad. Go to tutorial and read the textbook to learn. DO WEEKLY QUIZZES and you will be more than fine.
